steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: A linear model $y = b + mx$ requires matching two features of the scatterplot: the sign and magnitude of the slope, and the $y$-intercept.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y=0.8+8.5x$': positive slope, but the data trends downward. ✗
  • Choice B — '$y=0.8-8.5x$': negative slope, but $y$-intercept near $0.8$ is far too low — the data starts around $8.5$ at $x=0$, and a slope of $-8.5$ is far too steep. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y=8.5+0.8x$': positive slope, but the data trends downward. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y=8.5-0.8x$': $y$-intercept $\approx 8.5$ matches the data at $x=0$, and slope $-0.8$ gives a gentle downward trend consistent with the scatterplot. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
